channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple introduces the iPhone 16e, a fast and powerful mid-range smartphone
item.description: Apple’s new iPhone 16e combines three innovations in one $599 package: an A18 chip, Apple’s fast smartphone processor; Apple Intelligence, the company’s private-by-design take on artificial intelligence; and the C1, Apple’s first-ever 5G radio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: iPhone 16e live updates – Apple's next-gen budget iPhone is official
item.description: Apple has officially announced the iPhone 16e. Follow along as we react to the launch and summarize the key specs, release date, and price information.
item.dc:creator: philip.berne@futurenet.com (Philip Berne), TechRadar

item.title: AI hallucinations could get lawyers fired, law firm says
item.description: U.S. personal injury law firm Morgan & Morgan sent an urgent email this month to its more than 1,000 lawyers: Artificial intelligence can invent fake case law, and using made-up information in a court filing could get you fired.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Kai Cenat plans to launch a ‘streaming university’ to teach aspiring creators
item.description: Kai Cenat is launching a “streaming university.” Cenat announced his plans during a February 13 stream, explaining how he wants to help streamers both big and small learn from his success. “I’m going to rent out a university over a course of a weekend. It will be streaming university.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth, consumers
item.description: Since taking office, Trump has said he would place tariffs on foreign production of computer chips and semiconductors in order to return chip manufacturing to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cisco Execs Bullish on AI Growth Amid Strong Earnings
item.description: Cisco executives express optimism about the growth in artificial intelligence orders, with $700 million in sales so far this year and a projected $1 billion in 2025. The company reports strong earnings in the second quarter, driven by demand for networking products, including switching, enterprise routing, and industrial IoT applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
